title: Fix inner-layer copper pour rendering
description: test :https:pcb-viewer-1oocgd3ju-tscircuit.vercel.app?fixture7B22path223A22src2Fexamples2F20262Frepros2Finner-layer-copper-pours.fixture.tsx227D https:pcb-viewer-1oocgd3ju-tscircuit.vercel.app?fixture7B22path223A22src2Fexamples2F20262Frepros2Foverlapping-inner-layer-copper-pours.fixture.tsx227D render copper pours through the shared copper layer loop used by other copper elements while tracespadstext already looped across top, bottom, and inner1-inner6 remove the duplicate topbottom-only copper pour draw pass add an 8-layer repro fixture covering top, inner1-inner6, and bottom
body:
test :https://pcb-viewer-1oocgd3ju-tscircuit.vercel.app/?fixture=%7B%22path%22%3A%22src%2Fexamples%2F2026%2Frepros%2Finner-layer-copper-pours.fixture.tsx%22%7D

https://pcb-viewer-1oocgd3ju-tscircuit.vercel.app/?fixture=%7B%22path%22%3A%22src%2Fexamples%2F2026%2Frepros%2Foverlapping-inner-layer-copper-pours.fixture.tsx%22%7D
- render copper pours through the shared copper layer loop used by other copper elements while traces/pads/text already looped across top, bottom, and inner1-inner6
- remove the duplicate top/bottom-only copper pour draw pass
- add an 8-layer repro fixture covering `top`, `inner1`-`inner6`, and `bottom`

description: Removes unused methods and variables from the BoardGeomBuilder class to streamline the codebase and improve maintainability.

title: Add support for orientation for symbols with `_horz/_vert` similar to the `_(right|left|up|down)`
description: Adds logic to ensure schematic symbols with orientation suffixes (_horz_vert) maintain correct visual orientation after group placement rotations.
body:
### Motivation
- Ensure schematic symbols that include an orientation suffix (`_horz`/`_vert`) keep the correct visual orientation after group placement rotations. 

### Description
- Added logic in `lib/components/primitive-components/Group/Group_doInitialSchematicLayoutMatchPack.ts` to detect a symbol name suffix matching `_(horz|vert)`. 
- Compute a normalized rotation from `placement.ccwRotationDegrees` and, for 90° or 270° rotations, replace the suffix to swap `_horz` ↔ `_vert`. 
- Also kept the existing behavior that rotates text positions and direction suffixes like `_(right|left|up|down)`. 
- Updated the generated snapshot `tests/repros/__snapshots__/repro108-multiple-gunn-diode-fails-to-render-schematic.snap.svg` to reflect the adjusted layout/rotation output.

### Testing
- Ran the automated test suite with snapshot tests and updated the failing snapshot at `tests/repros/__snapshots__/repro108-multiple-gunn-diode-fails-to-render-schematic.snap.svg`; the test run succeeded.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69de2c8e86448327b87f5d57cbd610cb)

title: Support rotated pill-hole-with-rect-pad plated hole
description: Internally converts platedhole shapepill_hole_with_rect_pad pcbRotation...  to rotated_pill_hole_with_rect_pad, matching the existing rotated hole behavior, and adds reverse circuit-json mapping for compatibility.
body:
- internally convert `<platedhole shape="pill_hole_with_rect_pad" pcbRotation={...} />` to `rotated_pill_hole_with_rect_pad`, matching the existing rotated `hole` behavior
- add the reverse circuit-json mapping so `rotated_pill_hole_with_rect_pad` imports back into `PlatedHole` with `pcbRotation`
- keep rotated and non-rotated paths as separate explicit branches for readability
- add a regression test and snapshot for rotated pill-hole-with-rect-pad plated holes

title: Include vias in obstacle connectivity
description: Fixes autorouter obstacle generation so pcb_via obstacles include their own connectivity id instead of always using an empty connectedTo list.
body:
Fixes autorouter obstacle generation so pcb_via obstacles include their own connectivity id instead of always using an empty connectedTo list.

  Previously, vias were emitted as anonymous obstacles, unlike SMTPads and plated holes, which meant routed copper could not recognize them as connected copper in the simple-route obstacle model.

title: Add phased local autorouting by routingPhaseIndex
description: Implements phased local autorouting for groups, allowing higher-priority routes to be preserved by routing traces and nets in phases based on their routingPhaseIndex.
body:
Implements phased local autorouting for groups. Traces and nets are grouped by
  routingPhaseIndex, numbered phases route first, and null routes last. After each
  phase, routed trace geometry is added as obstacles for later phases so higher-priority
  routes are preserved.

title: Deduplicate logical ports from composite footprint copper
description: Fixes composite footprints where multiple copper primitives share the same logical pin, such as an SMT pad plus plated hole with the same portHints.
body:
Fixes composite footprints where multiple copper primitives share the same logical pin,
  such as an SMT pad plus plated hole with the same portHints.

  Previously, footprint port discovery returned one port per copper primitive, so repeated
  pin1/pin2 hints could inflate the inferred pin count and create extra logical pins like
  pin3 and pin4.

  This adds a shared helper to normalize footprint portHints, merge aliases, and dedupe
  ports by logical pin number for both string and JSX footprints.

title: Add click-to-inspect object labels for canvas rendering
description: Adds a debug toggle for enabling object interaction on the canvas, allowing users to click on labeled objects to view their labels, while hiding labels by default.
body:
## Summary
- hide canvas labels by default, including inline arrow labels, unless explicitly enabled
- add an `Enable Object Interation` debug toggle that opens a dialog when a labeled canvas object is clicked
- add shared canvas hit-testing for points, lines, arrows, polygons, circles, rects, and infinite lines
- cover the new hit-testing behavior with targeted tests

## Testing
- `bun test`
- `bun run build`

title: Refactor bus solver into focused helper modules
description: Summary extract boundary planning into BusBoundaryPlanner move shared bus solver types and preview-state utilities into dedicated modules pull pure path and goal-search helpers out of TinyHyperGraphBusSolver remove dead bus preview code and simplify solver coordination logic update bus routing tests to use the extracted boundary planner seam  Testing bun test testssolvercm5io-bus-routing.test.ts bunx tsc -p tsconfig.json --pretty false --ignoreDeprecations 5.0
body:
## Summary
- extract boundary planning into `BusBoundaryPlanner`
- move shared bus solver types and preview-state utilities into dedicated modules
- pull pure path and goal-search helpers out of `TinyHyperGraphBusSolver`
- remove dead bus preview code and simplify solver coordination logic
- update bus routing tests to use the extracted boundary planner seam

## Testing
- `bun test tests/solver/cm5io-bus-routing.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc -p tsconfig.json --pretty false --ignoreDeprecations 5.0`

title: Region Span Reproduction
description: Adds a new page for reproducing bus routing scenarios with a focus on region spans in the Tiny Hypergraph solver.

title: Add bus2
description: Adds a new bus configuration with multiple connection patches for PCB ports in the circuit design.

title: don't draw last candidate
description: Prevents the visualization of the last candidate in the solvers output when the solver has already found a solution.

title: Fix local high-density repair pushes
description: Track clearance conflicts at the route-point level so push cascades move only nearby overlapping segments instead of whole traces or unrelated route sections. Keep pushed routes eligible for later side processing, add tangential fallback moves, and reject full-route zero-clearance regressions so the cmn_3 top-left trace shifts without introducing overlaps. Improves autorouter results:
body: Track clearance conflicts at the route-point level so push cascades move only nearby overlapping segments instead of whole traces or unrelated route sections. Keep pushed routes eligible for later side processing, add tangential fallback moves, and reject full-route zero-clearance regressions so the cmn_3 top-left trace shifts without introducing overlaps. Improves autorouter results:

title: Expose Trace Clearance Violations with Live Solver Metrics and Visual Debug Markers
description: Adds real-time trace clearance validation to the high-density repair solver, enabling detection of trace-to-trace clearance conflicts and rendering visual markers for violations during repair iterations.
body: Introduce Trace Clearance Violations with visual support. Upgrades the high-density repair solver with real-time trace clearance validation, making hidden spacing issues immediately visible during repair iterations. Improvements Adds traceViolationCount to solver stats and output metrics Detects trace-to-trace clearance conflicts using configurable thresholds Filters same-net and via-only cases to focus on meaningful violations Renders visual trace violation markers directly in solver debug output Improves repair observability and debugging confidence

title: Add optional 'display_inductance' support to 'SourceSimpleInductor'

title: Add noConnect to chip props
description: Add a shared noConnect prop to ChipPropsSU and the chipProps zod schema, allowing callers to mark intentionally unconnected pins directly.
body:
## Summary
- Add a shared `noConnect` prop to `ChipPropsSU` and the `chipProps` zod schema.
- Type it against chip pin labels so callers can mark intentionally unconnected pins directly.
- Update generated README and prop docs to reflect the new API.

## Testing
- `bun test tests/chip1-basic.test.ts tests/chip3-type-tests.test.tsx`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- Ran the required generation scripts for component types, manual-edits docs, README docs, and props overview

title: Convert diode and LED categories to semantic tscircuit components
description: Adds support for LED components by converting diode and LED categories to semantic tscircuit components.

title: Convert diode-category EasyEDA parts to tscircuit diodes
description: Converts EasyEDA components categorized as diodes into diode components instead of generic chip components.
body:
Converts EasyEDA components categorized as diodes into <diode> components instead of generic <chip> components.

  Adds diode category detection from EasyEDA category/tag metadata, preserves the optional root category field in the schema, and emits DiodeProps output for generated diode components.

  Also remaps diode footprint port hints so EasyEDA anode/cathode labels align with tscircuit diode polarity (pin1/anode, pin2/cathode) instead of blindly preserving EasyEDA pin numbers.

title: Remove legacy pad geometry cleanup
description: Removes unused SMT pad manifold helper and legacy copper-pour state from BoardGeomBuilder as pads now render via textures, cleaning up dead code.
body:
## Summary
- remove the unused SMT pad manifold helper now that pads render via textures
- remove the no-op legacy copper-pour state and unused geometry accumulator from `BoardGeomBuilder`
- keep the shared rounded-rect prism helper because it is still used by hole and cutout geometry

## Why
Pads and copper pours no longer render as 3D board geometry in this path, so these leftover helpers and state-machine steps were dead code.

## Validation
- `npm run build`

title: Ignore local .codex workspace files
description: Adds .codex to .gitignore to prevent local workspace files from appearing as untracked files in the repository
body:
## Summary
- add `.codex` to `.gitignore`

## Why
The local `.codex` workspace file should not show up as an untracked file in the repository.

## Validation
- `npm run build`

title: Honor `noConnect` on chips: mark source ports do_not_connect and suppress missing-trace warnings
description: Allows a component-level noConnect list to mark source ports as intentionally unconnected and prevent spurious missing-trace warnings.
body:
### Motivation

- Allow a component-level `noConnect` list (e.g. on `chip`) to mark source ports as intentionally unconnected and prevent spurious missing-trace warnings.

### Description

- Skip creating a missing-trace warning if `db.source_port.get(...).do_not_connect` is true in `NormalComponent_doInitialSourceDesignRuleChecks.ts`.
- Extend `Port._getMatchingPinAttributes` to read the parent `_parsedProps.noConnect` array and produce a `{ doNotConnect: true }` match for aliases listed there, and return an empty array when no matches are found.
- Bump `@tscircuit/props` devDependency to `^0.0.506` and apply small test/typing cleanups in `intrinsic-jsx.ts` and component test fixtures.
- Add `tests/components/normal-components/chip-no-connect-prop.test.tsx` to verify `noConnect` behavior and adjust `tests/repros/repro87-trace-overlap.test.tsx` for updated typings.

### Testing

- Ran the unit test suite with `bun test` including `tests/components/normal-components/chip-no-connect-prop.test.tsx` and `tests/repros/repro87-trace-overlap.test.tsx`, and all tests passed.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69e19e20fbe8832eaac27f2512896a44)

title: Emit a warning when manufacturerPartNumber is missing for the connector
description: Adds a warning when the manufacturerPartNumber is missing for connectors with a specified standard, ensuring users are notified to provide this information to avoid future issues.

title: Add DRC error when routed trace is thinner than requested
description: Adds a routing check that reports a pcb_trace_error when a trace is routed thinner than its explicit requested thickness, making the issue visible to users.
body:
Motivation
Right now, if the autorouter cannot honor an explicit trace thickness because of clearance limits, it quietly routes a thinner trace with no feedback. That makes routing results misleading and hard to debug. Showing an error makes the problem visible and helps users understand that their requested thickness was not actually achieved.

This change adds a routing check that reports a pcb_trace_error when a trace is routed thinner than its explicit requested thickness. Instead of silently accepting a narrower routed trace, the checker now surfaces the mismatch as a DRC-style error.

description: Automated package update

title: Fix snapshot update workflow to commit changes when tests fail
description: Fixes the snapshot update workflow to ensure that changes are committed even when tests fail, by capturing exit codes without stopping the job.
body:
## Summary
- update the snapshot workflow to capture `bun test` exit codes without stopping the job before snapshot commits
- remove reliance on `continue-on-error` and explicitly exit successfully after recording the failure code
- preserve the later failure gate so the workflow still reports failed tests after committing snapshot updates

## Testing
- Not run (not requested)

title: Add files: [dist] to package.json
description: Adds the dist directory to the files array in package.json to specify which files should be included in the package distribution.

title: feat(parts-engine): implement class based approach and add platformFetch override for Easyeda
description: Adds platformFetch injection for EasyEDA-only fetchPartCircuitJson requests, with clear precedence (call-level - engine-level - global fetch), while keeping existing jlcPartsEngine usage backward-compatible via a default factory instance.
body: Adds platformFetch injection for EasyEDA-only fetchPartCircuitJson requests, with clear precedence (call-level -> engine-level -> global fetch), while keeping existing jlcPartsEngine usage backward-compatible via a default factory instance.

title: Fix via size conversion
description: Preserve explicit Circuit JSON via outer and drill diameters in KiCad export instead of clamping to hard-coded minimums.
body:
## Summary
- Preserve explicit Circuit JSON via outer and drill diameters in KiCad export instead of clamping to hard-coded minimums.
- Update the existing via-size repro to assert exact copy-through.
- Add a standalone via regression test for `0.4mm / 0.2mm`.

## Testing
- `bun test tests/pcb/repros/repro09-via-size-kicad-rules.test.tsx`
- `bun test tests/pcb/repros/repro11-route-via-size-copy.test.tsx`
- `bun test tests/pcb/repros/repro12-standalone-via-size-copy.test.tsx`

title: Fix Via Size Copying within pcb_trace.route
description: Fixes the issue where route-defined vias are not emitted, causing their sizes to be lost during the conversion process to KiCad.

title: Add robust color normalization and STEP color support for JSCAD models
description: Adds robust color normalization and support for STEP color representation in JSCAD models, enhancing color handling capabilities.

title: Redesign CAD viewer UI with collapsible panels, improved controls, and loading states
description: Redesigns the CAD viewer UI to include collapsible panels, enhanced controls, and loading states for better user interaction and experience.

title: Add loading viewport UI for model fetch and parsing states
description: Adds a loading viewport UI to indicate model fetch and parsing states to users.

title: More optimal centerline selection, fixes for greedy selection for follower traces
description: Fixes greedy selection for follower traces and implements a more optimal centerline selection algorithm in the bus routing solver.
body:
- **small fix to bus1 route**
- **Beam Search for more optimial bus solving**

title: Fix bus routing failures for large route sets and region-edge port classification
description: Fixes bus routing failures for large route sets and corrects region-edge port classification to avoid misassignments near corners.
body:
## Summary
- improve bus trace preview selection to favor candidates that leave viable remaining routes
- add large-route fallback logic for complete preview generation and queued candidate filtering to reduce inference failures
- correct serialized region port angle classification near corners to avoid assigning ports to the wrong edge
- extend the bus region-span regression coverage for repeated step execution

## Testing
- Not run (not requested)

title: Fast Follower-Style Bus Routing
description: This pull request introduces a new class, BusTraceInferencePlanner, which implements a fast follower-style bus routing algorithm. The class is designed to optimize the routing of buses in a hypergraph topology, allowing for more efficient pathfinding and trace generation. It includes various methods for building trace previews, evaluating trace usability, and managing trace segments, all aimed at improving the overall performance of the bus routing system.

title: Transform Trace Violation Debugging with Clustered Circle Markers for Clearer Visual Detection
description: Replaces rectangular trace violation markers with clustered circular indicators for improved visual detection in high-density layouts.
body: **PR Description:** This PR significantly improves trace-clearance debugging visuals by replacing rectangular violation markers with clustered circle indicators. ### Improvements * Replaces trace violation rectangles with cleaner circular markers * Clusters nearby violations into a single visual indicator to reduce noise * Scales marker size based on violation density * Moves markers into the circle overlay layer for clearer rendering * Improves readability when many conflicts occur in dense routing areas ### Impact Trace violations are now easier to spot, interpret, and debug at a glance—especially in crowded high-density layouts where rectangle overlays became noisy or hard to read.
